title: Wellington Cable Car Review
meta_desc: Discover the Wellington Cable Car experience with kids. Get the inside scoop on prices, age suitability, and tips for a fun family day out in Wellington.
intro: The Wellington Cable Car is a iconic attraction that's been delighting families since 1902. But what's it really like to ride the cable car with kids? We share our honest review, including tips on how to make the most of your visit. With a 9/10 family suitability rating, it's an experience not to be missed.

sections.heading: The Cable Car Experience
sections.body:
The Wellington Cable Car is a must-do experience for families visiting Wellington. The 4-minute ride takes you from Lambton Quay to the Botanic Garden at the top of the city, with stunning panoramic views of the harbour. Kids will love the excitement of the ride, and the scenic walk back down through the garden is a great way to burn off energy.

The cable car is suitable for all ages, from 0+, making it an excellent option for families with babies or toddlers. The duration of the ride is approximately 30 minutes, plus a 45-minute walk back down, so be sure to plan for some extra time.

sections.heading: Value for Money and Insider Tips
sections.body:
While the cable car experience is undeniably fun, it's worth considering the value for money. At $12 per adult and $6 per child, it may not be the cheapest activity, but the views and experience make it well worth the cost. To save money, consider visiting during the week or outside of peak season.

Insider tip: be sure to sit on the right-hand side of the cable car for the best views of the harbour. Also, take some time to explore the Botanic Garden at the top, which is free and offers some beautiful walks and playgrounds for kids.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit and Booking
sections.body: The best time to visit the Wellington Cable Car is during the week or outside of peak season, when prices are lower and the crowds are smaller. You can book your tickets online in advance, which is a good idea during peak season. Be sure to check the cable car's website for any special deals or discounts.
list: Book online in advance to save time and money
Check the website for special deals and discounts

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body: While the Wellington Cable Car is an excellent experience for families, there are a few downsides to consider. The cable car can be quite crowded during peak season, which may be overwhelming for young children. Additionally, the walk back down through the garden can be steep and uneven in some places, so be sure to bring a stroller or carrier for little ones.
